suggestion.headline: Repeat troponin rising further (4274→5100 ng/L); ACS pathway continues
suggestion.summary: 3-hour repeat Troponin I remains markedly elevated and has risen from 4274 ng/L to 5100 ng/L (ref <14), consistent with ongoing/evolving myocardial injury rather than resolution. Patient is already on the ACS pathway with Cardiology involved and on aspirin 75 mg, bisoprolol, atorvastatin, lisinopril and PRN GTN. Per trust ACS guidance (v3.1), this trend supports continuing serial troponin monitoring, continuous cardiac (telemetry) monitoring, and urgent Cardiology review to determine invasive vs conservative strategy. No new anomaly flag is being raised here — this is a continuation/re-assessment of the previously actioned critical result — but the lack of improvement means the corrective pathway should continue rather than be stood down. Any escalation of antiplatelet/anticoagulant therapy (e.g. second antiplatelet, enoxaparin 1 mg/kg per pathway) remains a decision for the responsible clinician after reviewing bleeding risk and renal function.

proposedActions.title: Cardiology Review
proposedActions.detail: Troponin continuing to rise (4274→5100 ng/L) 3 hours apart; per ACS pathway, urgent Cardiology review to determine invasive vs conservative strategy — Cardiology already managing, recommend re-review in light of rising trend.

proposedActions.title: Continue Continuous Cardiac Monitoring
proposedActions.detail: Per ACS pathway guidance, continuous cardiac (telemetry) monitoring should continue given the non-resolving troponin rise.

proposedActions.title: Schedule Next Troponin Recheck
proposedActions.detail: Serial Troponin I recommended per ACS pathway; schedule the next recheck to confirm whether the rise is plateauing or continuing to evolve.

trace.observation: Aspirin 75 mg (active); Lisinopril 10 mg (active); Atorvastatin 40 mg (active); Bisoprolol 2.5 mg (active); GTN spray 400 mcg (active)

Acute Coronary Syndrome — Initial Management (v3.1): Confirmed or high-suspicion ACS: aspirin 300 mg loading dose (if no contraindication), a second antiplatelet per local pathway, and anticoagulation such as enoxaparin 1 mg/kg subcutaneously after assessing renal function and bleeding risk. Serial Troponin I (repeat at 3 hours), continuous cardiac monitoring, and urgent Cardiology review to determine invasive vs conservative strategy. Every drug and dose is confirmed by the responsible clinician before administration.
